description: The Chaldean mafia is a collective of criminal organizations composed of Chaldeans that emerged in the United States during the 1980s. It is active primarily in Detroit, as well as San Diego and Phoenix. The groups are involved in murder, arson, weapons trafficking, narcotics trafficking, money laundering, fraud, theft, counterfeiting, racketeering, alien smuggling, assault, kidnapping, and armed robbery. The organization was founded by Louis Akrawi, known as the 'Godfather of the Chaldean Mafia', who fled Iraq in 1968.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Detroit, Chaldean criminals are involved in distributing Canadian MDMA...
country_links.context: The Chaldean mafia distributes Canadian MDMA in Detroit.

country_links.evidence_quote: Chaldean organized crime groups are involved in... with links to... the Sinaloa Cartel and the Mexican Mafia... In Detroit, Chaldean criminals are involved in distributing... Mexican marijuana...
country_links.context: The Chaldean mafia distributes Mexican marijuana and has links to the Sinaloa Cartel and Mexican Mafia.
